products.notes: Primary Studio web client. Built output is mirrored into ui_static and served by the legacy Flask UI layer.
Developer-facing product client previously stored under ui_studio. Managed as its own nested Git repo.
Electron desktop client. Managed as its own nested Git repo. Domain reserved for any future public-facing Home surface.
Security product. Public-facing brand remains vuln.ai. Managed as its own nested Git repo.

legacy_surfaces.notes: Older Python/Flask Studio shell still wired into services, scripts, docs, and startup paths. Migrate-first, not removable yet.

repo_policy.nested_repo_rule: ORI Dev, ORI Home, and ORI Red are currently maintained as nested repos inside the platform workspace. Edit and commit them within their own repos until an explicit flattening or submodule decision is made.
repo_policy.flattening_rule: Do not flatten or absorb nested product repos into the platform repo implicitly. Make that a deliberate migration with history and deploy implications reviewed first.

domain_policy.shared_api_notes: Shared API/auth/runtime surface. Keep stable because it is already wired into JIT auth and first-party clients.
rules: Customer-facing ORI products should use explicit product subdomains, not generic nouns.
dev.thynaptic.com is reserved for internal dev, staging, previews, or scratch services, not the ORI Dev product.
Public brand aliases like vuln.ai can coexist with canonical product domains.
